---
draft: false
title: "Climate–Decent Work Risk Assessment"
description: "Interactive Tableau dashboard combining IPCC-framework climate hazards with ten decent work indicators across Brazilian agricultural municipalities."
date: 2026-02-20
highlight: true
categories: [Interactive, Climate Change, Decent Work, CLIDEWO, Tableau]
image: img/cover.png
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
---

[← Back to Portfolio](../../portfolio.html){.back-link}

::: {.label}
Interactive · Climate–Decent Work · Brazil · CLIDEWO
:::

**Which regions experience the highest extreme heat exposure combined with excessive working hours? Which population groups face the most severe decent-work deficits? Which crops carry the highest agrochemical risk?**

The dashboard lets you combine climate hazards and decent work indicators to answer your own questions.

Our assessment adapts the IPCC climate risk framework — **hazards × exposure × vulnerability** — to examine climate risks through a decent work lens. Decent work is treated as a multi-dimensional concept comprising ten core elements linked to the five strategic pillars of the ILO's decent work agenda.

::: {.column-page}

```{=html}
<div class='tableauPlaceholder' id='viz1771582084225' style='position: relative'>
  <noscript>
    <a href='#'>
      <img alt='clidewo_interactive (web)' src='https://public.tableau.com/static/images/in/interactive_clidewo_climate_hazards/clidewo_interactiveweb/1_rss.png' style='border: none' />
    </a>
  </noscript>
  <object class='tableauViz' style='display:none;'>
    <param name='host_url' value='https%3A%2F%2Fpublic.tableau.com%2F' />
    <param name='embed_code_version' value='3' />
    <param name='site_root' value='' />
    <param name='name' value='interactive_clidewo_climate_hazards/clidewo_interactiveweb' />
    <param name='tabs' value='no' />
    <param name='toolbar' value='yes' />
    <param name='static_image' value='https://public.tableau.com/static/images/in/interactive_clidewo_climate_hazards/clidewo_interactiveweb/1.png' />
    <param name='animate_transition' value='yes' />
    <param name='display_static_image' value='yes' />
    <param name='display_spinner' value='yes' />
    <param name='display_overlay' value='yes' />
    <param name='display_count' value='yes' />
    <param name='language' value='en-GB' />
  </object>
</div>
<script type='text/javascript'>
  var divElement = document.getElementById('viz1771582084225');
  var vizElement = divElement.getElementsByTagName('object')[0];
  if (divElement.offsetWidth > 800) {
    vizElement.style.width = '1366px';
    vizElement.style.height = '795px';
  } else if (divElement.offsetWidth > 500) {
    vizElement.style.width = '1366px';
    vizElement.style.height = '795px';
  } else {
    vizElement.style.width = '100%';
    vizElement.style.height = '3177px';
  }
  var scriptElement = document.createElement('script');
  scriptElement.src = 'https://public.tableau.com/javascripts/api/viz_v1.js';
  vizElement.parentNode.insertBefore(scriptElement, vizElement);
</script>
```

:::

## Climate hazards covered

The assessment covers five climate hazards with documented links to occupational health and decent work outcomes: **extreme heat**, **flooding**, **drought**, **wildfires**, and **agrochemical exposure**. Each hazard is scored at the municipality level for Brazil, then crossed with worker exposure and sectoral vulnerability data.

## Decent work dimensions

Ten decent work elements are included, organised around the ILO's five strategic pillars: employment opportunities, rights at work, social protection, social dialogue, and conditions of work. Measurable indicators include wages, job security, working hours, occupational safety, and access to formal employment contracts.

## About CLIDEWO

This dashboard is a core output of the [CLIDEWO](https://www.clidewo.com) project — *Climate Change, Decent Work and Workers' Health: Vulnerabilities and Response in Brazilian Agriculture* — funded by the British Academy's ODA Challenge-Oriented Research Grants programme.
